algorithms.title: Cardiac Arrest Drug Administration Algorithm
algorithms.description: Decision framework for selecting and administering the correct drugs based on the cardiac arrest rhythm, including dose, timing, route, and flush protocol.
steps: Cardiac arrest confirmed → begin CPR and establish IV/IO access.
Determine rhythm category: Shockable (VF/pVT) or Nonshockable (Asystole/PEA).
SHOCKABLE (VF/pVT): Deliver first shock immediately → resume CPR.
After 2nd shock: Epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO (1:10,000), push during compressions, flush with 10–20 mL NS + fluid push. Repeat q3–5 min.
If VF/pVT persists after epinephrine: Amiodarone 300 mg IV/IO (preferred) OR Lidocaine 1–1.5 mg/kg IV/IO (alternative). Flush after administration.
If VF/pVT continues: Second antiarrhythmic dose — Amiodarone 150 mg IV/IO or Lidocaine 0.5–0.75 mg/kg IV/IO.
Give Epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO (1:10,000) as soon as feasible — ideally within 3–5 min. Repeat q3–5 min.
SYSTEM-SPECIFIC DRUGS: Hyperkalemia → Calcium + Insulin/Glucose + NaHCO₃ 1 mEq/kg. TCA OD → NaHCO₃ 1 mEq/kg. Torsades → Magnesium 1–2 g IV.
ALWAYS: After every drug bolus → 10–20 mL NS flush → IV/IO fluid push. Position extremity if peripheral IV.
NEVER: Use adenosine in any pulseless rhythm. Never use epinephrine 1:1,000. Never dilute amiodarone in normal saline (standard amiodarone must be diluted in D5W).
